name: Tâb
author: Abstract Games factory
description: Tâb, the Middle-Eastern 'running-fight' dice-war game — the root of the tâb/sîg family — in the ruleset Edward Lane recorded in 1820s Cairo. Four stick dice throw 1 ('tâb'), 2, 3, 4 or 6; a 1, 4 or 6 earns another throw, and the whole banked chain is then spent in any order. Each piece is a dead 'Christian' until a tâb converts it to a 'Muslim'; converted pieces run your home row, then loop the two middle rows forever, with a once-only raid through the enemy's home row. Land on enemy pieces to capture the whole pile; land on your own to build a stack that moves as one, splits only with a tâb, and is cut to a single piece if it doubles back. Capture ALL enemy pieces to win.
